chapter.title: MantisBT REST API
chapter.para:
The primary means of integrating with MantisBT with web services is with the bundled REST API, which is accessible at
https://server.com/mantis/api/rest/.

section.title: Authentication
section.para: REST API calls can be authenticated using one of the following methods:

listitem.para: The primary authentication method. Create an API token for your user account and pass it in the Authorization HTTP request header. API token authentication is required when using impersonation.
When anonymous access is enabled and an anonymous account is configured, the API can be accessed without authentication for read-only operations.
Used internally by the MantisBT Web UI when JavaScript calls the API on behalf of an already authenticated user session.

section.title: Impersonation
section.para: Users with sufficient access (controlled by the $g_impersonate_user_threshold configuration option, default: ADMINISTRATOR) can make REST API calls on behalf of another user. Typical use cases include:

listitem.para: Reproducing an issue as reported by a specific user
Testing a user's access to projects, issues, and fields
Performing operations on behalf of a service account

section.para: To impersonate a user, include the X-Mantis-Username HTTP request header with the username of the target user. The following constraints apply:

listitem.para: The caller must authenticate with an API token (the Authorization header). Impersonation is not available with cookie or anonymous authentication.
The caller must have the access level defined by $g_impersonate_user_threshold.
A user cannot impersonate another user whose access level is higher than their own.
The target user must be a valid, enabled user.

section.title: Response Headers
section.para: Every REST API response includes the following informational headers:

listitem.para: The username under which the request was executed. When impersonation is active, this reflects the impersonated user's username rather than the caller's.
The authentication method used for the request. Possible values are api-token, cookie, and anonymous.

section.title: Postman
section.para:
Postman is a tool to enable REST API development and collaboration. It is used by MantisBT team to share the API definitions and enables the community to experiment with the API.
Download Postman.

Once Postman is installed, you should be able to bootstrap it with the MantisBT API collection via the MantisBT API documentation posted on Postman.
Download Collection.

The MantisBT API Postman collection is also distributed with MantisBT in the `api/rest/api.postman_collection.json`. The Postman environment template is also available at `api/rest/env.postman_environment.json`.

section.title: Exporting Postman Collection
section.para: The bundled Postman collection can be updated by clicking on the "..." next to "Mantis Bug Tracker REST API" and choosing "Export...". Then select "Collection v2.1" and click "Export" and select `api/rest/api.postman_collection.json`. Then follow the standard pull request and checkin process.
